conditions: Benign prostatic hyperplasia (prostate enlargement) with urinary symptoms
health.mental_health: In sustained recovery from alcohol use disorder; attends weekly support meetings and outpatient counseling. Ongoing anxiety about relapse and shame-related depression symptoms while rebuilding trust with family; sleep can be patchy during stressful weeks.
recent_visits: Urology follow-up at Charité for urinary symptoms and PSA monitoring (last month)
Diabetes check-in with Hausarzt in Neukölln including HbA1c and kidney function labs (6 weeks ago)
Physiotherapy session for knee osteoarthritis at a Praxis near Hermannplatz (this week)
Podiatry visit for diabetic foot screening and callus care (2 months ago)

finance.income_range: €2,100–€2,600 net per month (variable with shifts and weekend work)
finance.debt: Repaying ~€7,800 in consumer debt accumulated during the worst period of drinking (overdraft and a small personal loan); on a structured repayment plan and trying to avoid missed payments.
investments: Small emergency savings in a Tagesgeld account
recent_transactions: Monthly rent transfer for 1-bedroom flat in Neukölln (€980 warm)
Payment to debt consolidation/repayment plan provider (€220)
Grocery purchase at Lidl and Turkish market (~€68)

relationships: Older sister, Megan Anderson (lives in Hamburg) — cautiously supportive, regular check-ins tied to recovery
Adult child, Jamie Anderson (lives in Berlin) — relationship strained by past broken promises; slowly rebuilding with planned sober meetups
Sponsor from a Berlin recovery group, Rainer K. — primary accountability contact
Close friend and neighbor, Aylin Demir — helps with practical support and accompanies Quinn to some appointments
Ex-partner, Lars M. — limited contact; boundaries around past conflict and relapse triggers
social.social_media_activity: Keeps a low profile; uses WhatsApp daily and Facebook rarely (mostly to follow Berlin community groups). Avoids posting about recovery publicly and has tightened privacy settings after a past oversharing incident.
private_conversations: Apologies and restitution plans related to past alcohol-related incidents and missed family events
Disclosure of relapse triggers (certain bars, paydays, loneliness after late shifts)
Discussion of urinary symptoms and embarrassment about prostate issues with a trusted friend
Planning boundaries with ex-partner and how to handle contact without jeopardizing sobriety

schedule.work_hours: Shift-based home care: typically 07:00–15:00 or 13:00–21:00, 5 days/week; occasional weekend morning visits and one on-call evening every other week
regular_appointments: Weekly recovery meeting (AA-style) Tuesdays 19:00 near Kottbusser Tor
Outpatient counseling session every Thursday 17:30 (addiction aftercare)
Blood glucose check and medication planning every Sunday evening
Monthly Hausarzt appointment or lab visit as scheduled (usually first week of the month)
upcoming_events: Urology ultrasound/uroflow test appointment next Wednesday 08:10 at Charité
Work in-service training on dementia care next Friday 10:00–12:00
Coffee meetup with Jamie (adult child) this Saturday 11:30 at a café in Kreuzberg (planned as a sober, time-limited visit)
Rent and debt repayment transfers scheduled for the 1st of next month
schedule.daily_routine: Early breakfast with medication, checks blood glucose before leaving. Commutes by U-Bahn to clients, documents care notes after each visit, and keeps evenings structured (walk, simple dinner, meeting or reading) to avoid isolation and relapse triggers. Sets phone alarms for meds, hydration, and bedtime.

professional.company: A mid-sized ambulant care service (Pflegedienst) in Berlin serving Neukölln/Kreuzberg clients; works under a team lead nurse and coordinates with family caregivers
projects: Completing annual hygiene and medication-handling refresher training
Standardizing daily documentation templates for two high-need clients to reduce missed entries
Building a mobility-support routine for an elderly client with fall risk (in coordination with physiotherapy instructions)
professional.performance_issues: Previously had one formal warning last year for a late arrival and incomplete documentation during early recovery; currently stable with improved punctuality and uses a peer buddy at work for accountability when stress is high.
workplace_relationships: Supportive team lead nurse who checks in discreetly about workload and reliability
One coworker who is skeptical due to past absence patterns; relationship improving as trust rebuilds
Good rapport with several client families, who request Quinn specifically for calm communication

hobbies: Long walks along the Landwehrkanal and in Tempelhofer Feld
Cooking simple, budget-friendly meals (soups, roasted vegetables, fish when affordable)
Listening to podcasts and audiobooks on commutes
Visiting museums on discounted days (when energy and budget allow)
habits: Tracks blood glucose and tries to keep regular meal times
Avoids alcohol and keeps a written relapse-prevention plan; deletes bar ads and avoids certain streets after late shifts
Uses nicotine gum occasionally during cravings (trying not to replace one addiction with another)
Keeps a strict sleep routine on non-late-shift days to manage mood and pain
Meal-preps on Sundays to reduce impulsive takeaway spending
lifestyle.living_situation: Rents a modest 1-bedroom flat in Neukölln; living alone after separation. Keeps the home intentionally alcohol-free and has a small corner set up for journaling and recovery reading. Neighbor Aylin has an extra key for emergencies.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Diabetes-conscious and joint-friendly: aims for higher fiber, fewer sugary drinks, and smaller portions of bread/pasta. Tries to limit processed foods and salt for blood pressure; allows occasional treats when stress is managed without alcohol.
